{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Water being added to concrete mix on site without authorization, altering slump beyond approved mix design",
    "Unapproved water addition increases water-cement ratio, reducing compressive strength and durability of placed concrete",
    "Violation of ACI 301 and ACI 304 requirements for mix design control and field acceptance procedures",
    "Concrete placed with manipulated slump is non-compliant with approved submittal and may not pass third-party inspection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt water addition to the concrete mix",
    "Stop placement of any concrete that has had unapproved water added",
    "Notify the ready-mix supplier, general contractor, and third-party inspector of the non-compliant water addition",
    "Quarantine and remove any concrete already placed with added water for evaluation or removal and replacement",
    "Verify slump with proper cone test per ASTM C143 and compare against approved mix design tolerances",
    "Document the incident with batch tickets, delivery tickets, and field observations for corrective action review"
  ],
  "rationale": "Adding water on site to manipulate slump is a serious non-compliance that directly compromises the structural integrity of the concrete by increasing the water-cement ratio beyond design parameters. ACI 301 and ACI 304 strictly govern mix design control and prohibit unauthorized water addition. Any concrete placed with added water is defective and non-compliant, requiring immediate stoppage and remediation.",
  "references": ["ACI 301", "ACI 304", "ASTM C143"]
}
